body:
MORNING INTEL — 2026-07-13 (snapshot 2026-07-13_13)
Regime data: VIX 17.06 | HY OAS 269 | Breadth 63.6%
AMZN: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
NVDA: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
SPY: TRADE | NEUTRAL conf 51.666666666666664% | Range 729.21–769.13 | iron_condor x1
Portfolio risk after fills: pending Artha confirmation
Discipline: 30d system PnL $0 | override PnL $0
Data quality: all sources OK

structured.takeaway: The current regime shows elevated volatility with VIX at 17.06, a steep contango between the 9-day (14.87) and 3-month (19.61) implied vols, and a high HY OAS of 269 bps signaling credit stress, while breadth at 63.6% and the DXY at 101.30 reflect cautious risk appetite amid geopolitical tensions. SPY at $749.17 with IV at 26.07% and negative sentiment (-2) is vulnerable to further downside given the Nasdaq’s sharp selloff driven by escalating U.S.-Iran conflict, which remains the key near-term catalyst to watch as it could trigger risk-off flows and push yields higher (US10Y 4.56%). NVDA at $203.53 with 50.22% IV and positive sentiment (+1) stands out as a relative winner, supported by sector rotation into semis amid generational shifts favoring NVIDIA and Micron, while AMZN at $247.31 with 41.33% IV and positive sentiment (+1) is a compelling long given it’s the cheapest in over a decade, suggesting value entry despite broader market weakness. The primary risk is a renewed geopolitical escalation that could exacerbate volatility and derail the fragile inflation narrative, especially with a hot inflation print expected this week that might prompt another Fed hike, as flagged by Waller. System status: trade signal gates are fully active, enabling aggressive positioning aligned with these directional biases.

SPY.sentiment_driver: Nasdaq closes sharply lower as stocks fall

NVDA.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

AMZN.sentiment_driver: Amazon Stock Hasn't Been This Cheap in Over a Decade
AMZN.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

news.title: Nasdaq closes sharply lower as stocks fall after latest U.S.-Iran escalation
Nasdaq leading stocks lower in final hour as U.S.-Iran tensions escalate — live
Inflation to fall for first time in 6 years. Consumers won't see much relief.
A Generational Transfer Is Happening: Amazon, Google, Microsoft Are the Losers. NVIDIA and Micron the Winners.
Amazon Stock Hasn't Been This Cheap in Over a Decade. Has the Sell-Off Gone Too Far?
A hot inflation reading this week could mean a rate hike soon, Fed’s Waller says
Nasdaq and S&P 500 open lower as Iran launches new strikes
Why Citigroup is the one to watch when banks report earnings this week
Economic calendar: Treasury balance today; CPI and PPI this week
India's inflation accelerates to 4.38% in June, exceeding forecasts
The stock-market rally now hinges more on AI than oil
Oil prices rise, stock futures dip after latest flare-up of U.S.-Iran strikes

narratives.this_week: Bank earnings dominate this week with major reports from BAC, JPM, WFC, and GS on July 14. Investors will focus on credit quality and margin trends amid a stable VIX at 17.06 and HY OAS near 269 bps, which suggest moderate risk appetite. Health care also draws attention with JNJ and UNH earnings, where UNH’s bullish outlook could provide defensive support to SPY amid broader financial sector uncertainty. Watch for any signs of stress or resilience in financials that could ripple into tech and consumer discretionary sectors, including AMZN and NVDA.
narratives.next_week: Next week features key tech and industrial earnings, notably GOOGL and TSLA on July 22, alongside real estate and materials names like CCI and NEM. GOOGL’s bullish sentiment is critical for communication services and could bolster SPY’s tech exposure, while TSLA’s neutral stance tempers enthusiasm in consumer discretionary. Industrial reports from RTX and HON will provide insight into manufacturing demand, potentially influencing cyclical sectors and commodity-linked stocks, which in turn affect broader market sentiment and risk appetite.
narratives.next_month: The following month brings heavy hitters MSFT, AMZN, and AAPL on July 29-30, alongside the FOMC meeting and July PCE data. The FOMC’s likely hold at 3.50% with a small chance of a cut, combined with cautious inflation expectations, sets a delicate backdrop for tech and consumer discretionary earnings. Positive beats from MSFT, AMZN, and AAPL could drive SPY higher and reinforce momentum in NVDA, while any softness in inflation or guidance may weigh on growth stocks. Investors should also monitor AMD’s earnings for AI-related revenue signals, which have broader implications for NVDA’s market share and sector leadership.
